title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: P7M8 The Heckler & Koch P7 is an innovative German pistol chambered for 9x19mm.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: It was used by Karla Fry in a bank robbery and later an attempt to kill Andrew Bogomil .

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The most popular varieties of the P7 were the P7M8 (so-named for its 8-round single stack magazine) and the P7M13 (so-named for its 13-round double stack magazine).

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: This gun was submitted into the United States Army pistol trials to compete for the military's sidearm, but failed to win the contract.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: Design [ ] The P7's odd, slanted appearance of the handle is because there is an extension underneath the trigger.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: This extention is an experimental technology called a "handle cocker", which allows you to cock the gun without having to pull the slide or hammer back.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The P7 has been dubbed the "self-loading pistol" because of the handle mounted safety and usually costs around $1,000.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: So the exotic gun is appropriate for an upper class terrorist like Gruber because of its expensive price.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: There are numerous variants of the HK P7.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: These include the PSP (Police Selfloading Pistol), P7, P7PT8, P7M13, P7M8, P7K3, P7M10 and the P7M7.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The P7M7 was an extremely rare .45 ACP variant that was discontinued after prototype stages because it was too expensive and impractical to manufacture.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: It had a seven round magazine capacity.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The P7M10 was chambered for .40 S&W, and had a ten round magazine capacity.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The P7M8, P7M13, P7 and PSP pistols are all chambered in 9x19mm Parabellum.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The M8 has an eight round single stack magazine and the M13 (this pistol) boasts a thirteen round double stack magazine capacity.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The P7PT8 was a training firearm that was never popular, mainly because it couldn't fire live ammunition, only special training rounds.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The P7K3 was a conversion pistol kit which had interchangeable barrels and slides for different calibers such as .380 ACP, .22 LR and .32 ACP.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: None of these pistols remain in production; all models having been discontinued in 2008.

title: Heckler & Koch P7M13
sentence_text: The P7 saw widespread service around the world, in countries such as Saudi Arabia.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: The S&W Model 669 is a Smith & Wesson second-generation semi-automatic pistol, chambered for the 9x19mm cartridge.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: It is a compact, DA/SA pistol with a stainless steel slide and aluminum frame.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: History and design [ ] The 669 is an early model in Smith and Wesson's well-regarded 59-series, envisioned as pistols that could be easily concealed, but possessed sufficient firepower to serve as service weapons as well.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: It was a traditional double-action weapon with a 3.5-inch barrel, equipped with a slide-mounted safety/decocker.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: Magazine capacity was 12 rounds.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: When Smith and Wesson introduced its "third-generation" series of semiautomatics, several similar pistols succeeded the Model 669.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: These included the Model 6904, which had an alloy frame with black finish, the Model 6906, which was the same pistol in a stainless steel finish, both operating in double-action/single-action mode.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: The Model 6946 was similar to the 6906 but operated in double-action only mode with a consistent heavy trigger pull that was directed towards law enforcement (the bobbed hammer on the 6906 could be cocked into single-action mode) Although the 69-series of pistol is no longer produced, the polymer-framed Smith & Wesson M&P compact possesses similar dimensions, and the same barrel length and magazine capacity (in 9mm).

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: The 669 is often referred to as the 'little brother' of the 659 and will even accept the 14 round magazine of the 659.

title: Smith & Wesson 669
sentence_text: Some other features of the gun is the 'no fire' without a magazine and the 'safe' half cock mode (if you pull the trigger lightly, the hammer will go half way back and lock) and de-cocking lever/safety on the slide.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
sentence_text: The Beretta 92FS Inox is a 9x19mm semi-automatic handgun seen in Beverly Hills Cop III .

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
sentence_text: It is essentially a Beretta 92FS constructed out of stainless steel with satin finish rather than steel with blued finish.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
sentence_text: Description [ ] The Inox models (from the French "inoxidable" meaning non-oxidizing) feature the following parts made in anodized stainless steel with a light bead blasting finish rather than blued carbon steel: the barrel, the slide (including the extractor, the safety and the right-side manual safety lever), the trigger (and trigger pin), and slide stop lever.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
sentence_text: The older Italian-made Beretta Inoxes are usually better received than the American-made Inoxes, which feature blued controls, giving them a semi two-tone look.

title: Beretta 92FS Inox
sentence_text: The Inox models are some of the most popular Berettas and are still produced today.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: The Smith & Wesson Model 27 is the original .357 Magnum revolver and was first produced in 1935, and many versions of it are still in production today with distinctive high-gloss finishes.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: The Model 27 was built on Smith & Wesson's carbon steel, large N-frame, and was available at various times with 31⁄2", 4", 5", 6" or 83⁄8" barrel lengths and had adjustable sights.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: It was seen in Beverly Hills Cop II .

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: Description [ ] When first introduced by Smith & Wesson in 1935 it was known as the Registered Magnum.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: The model was essentially a custom order revolver.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: Barrel lengths could be had in quarter-inch increments from 3.5" to 8.75" in length.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: In addition to the different lengths of barrels available there were different grips, front sights, triggers, hammers, and finishes available.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: Each Registered Magnum came with a certificate of authenticity.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: Even though it was introduced in the middle of the Great Depression and was extremely expensive, Smith & Wesson found itself backlogged with orders for the four years that it produced the Registered Magnum.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: The Kansas City Police Department issued the Registered Magnum to its officers, and many other law enforcement officers across the United States carried the Registered Magnum.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: In 1939 Smith & Wesson stopped producing the Registered Magnum.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: It was replaced with the .357 Magnum.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: The .357 Magnum was available with barrel lengths of 3.5", 5", 6.5" and 8.75".

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: It has been reported that these were the most popular barrel lengths for the Registered Magnum.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: Essentially the .357 Magnum was still the Registered Magnum, but standardized for ease of production and economy.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: The Smith & Wesson Model 28 "Highway Patrolman" was introduced as a lower cost version of the Model 27 in 1954, stripped of some of the features of the Model 27, such as polishing.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: It was noted for its durability and reliability.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: The 31⁄2" barrel length was extremely popular with FBI agents from the 1940s through the 1960s.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: Skeeter Skelton considered the Model 27 with a 5" barrel as the best all around handgun.

title: Smith & Wesson Model 27
sentence_text: General George Patton carried an ivory handled Registered Magnum with a 31⁄2 inch barrel (along with his ivory handled Colt Peacemaker); Patton called the Model 27 his "killing gun." title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Michael "Mikey" Tandino Status Deceased Profession Small-time crook Security guard at Hollis Benton Art Gallery Affiliation Axel Foley Jenny Summers Portrayed By James Russo Michael "Mikey" Tandino was a small-time crook and a close friend of Axel Foley from Beverly Hills Cop .

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Contents 1 Biography 1 Biography 1.1 Early Life 1.1 Early Life 1.2 Beverly Hills Cop 1.2 Beverly Hills Cop 2 Trivia 2 Trivia Biography [ ] Early Life [ ] Michael Tandino grew up with Axel Foley and Jenny Summers in the same Detroit neighborhood.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: In their teenage years, he and Foley would commit all sorts of mischief, including the criminal kind.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: At the age of fifteen, they stole a Cadillac, but were stopped by the police.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Tandino was sentenced to youth custody, but out of loyalty to his friend he kept Foley safe from persecution.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: It was presumably this event that turned Axel's life around and got him started as a police detective.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Mikey was arrested again for breaking into homes and sentenced to a longer prison stay.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Beverly Hills Cop [ ] As an adult, Tandino evidently continued to live of small-time schemes until he was recommended by Summers to work as a security guard for the art dealer Victor Maitland in Beverly Hills.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: However, sometime before he went back to Detroit, he chanced upon a crate containing German bearer bonds lying unattended in one of Maitland's warehouses, and took some of the bonds with him.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: However, he was unaware that these bonds were part of a smuggling operation run by Maitland, who took extreme exception to the theft and sent his chief bodyguard Zack after Tandino.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Tandino after meeting his end Arriving in Detroit, Tandino broke into Foley's apartment to surprise his old friend.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Upon their reunion, Tandino and Foley went out carousing, in which course Tandino told Foley about his new job and the bearer bonds he had swiped.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: When they arrived back at Foley's apartment, however, they found Zack and Casey waiting for them; Casey knocked Foley out, while Zack retrieved the stolen bonds and then executed Tandino by shooting him twice in the back of his head.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Angered by the murder, Foley later went to Beverly Hills to investigate and avenge his best friend's death, setting the events of the film series in motion.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: Trivia [ ] Mikey was released from prison a year early for good behavior.

title: Mikey Tandino
sentence_text: His crimes included stealing cars and breaking into houses.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Paul Guilfoyle (born April 28, 1949) is an American television and film actor.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He was a regular cast member of the forensic television drama CSI: Crime Scene Investigation where he played Captain Jim Brass.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He played Nikos Thomopolis in Beverly Hills Cop II .

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Life and Career [ ] Guilfoyle was born in Canton, Massachusetts.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He attended Boston College High School and spoke at the 2005 commencement of the school's seniors.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Guilfoyle enrolled at Lehigh University in 1968.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He eventually graduated from Yale in 1977 with a major in economics.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He studied at the Actor's Studio before building up a substantial theatrical reputation on and off Broadway, including 12 years with the Theatre Company of Boston, appearances in David Rabe's The Basic Training of Pavlo Hummel with Al Pacino and in David Mamet's Glengarry Glen Ross.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He is often mistakenly referred to as the son of character actor Paul Guilfoyle but they are not related.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Guilfoyle appeared in Howard the Duck, and in an early episode of Crime Story, playing a criminal who takes a hostage, getting into a shootout with the Major Crimes Unit.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He has since become one of the industry's leading character actors, specializing in roles on both the good and bad side of law enforcement.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: His television appearances most notably include guest roles on Miami Vice, Law & Order, New York Undercover, Ally McBeal and Justice League Unlimited as Travis Morgan, the Warlord.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: His film credits are numerous, spanning nearly three decades.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: His appearances in notable films include Three Men and a Baby, Wall Street, Celtic Pride, Beverly Hills Cop II, Quiz Show, Hoffa, Mrs.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Doubtfire, Air Force One, Striptease, Amistad, The Negotiator, Extreme Measures, Session 9, Primary Colors and L.A.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Confidential.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Guilfoyle also appears in Alter Bridge's video for their single "Broken Wings", and the HBO original movie Live from Baghdad.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Guilfoyle is best known for his role as L.V.P.D.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Captain James "Jim" Brass in the crime drama CSI: Crime Scene Investigation, a role he had since the show's inception in 2000.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: In 2014 it was announced that Guilfoyle was to leave CSI: Crime Scene Investigation after 14 years in the role.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: Guilfoyle lives in New York City with his family.

title: Paul Guilfoyle
sentence_text: He is fluent in French.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: Robert Clark "Bob" Seger' (born May 6, 1945) is an American rock singer-songwriter, guitarist and pianist.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: His song "Shakedown" was featured in the Beverly Hills Cop II soundtrack as the primary song for the film Biography [ ] As a locally successful Detroit-area artist, he performed and recorded as Bob Seger and the Last Heard and Bob Seger System throughout the 1960s.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: By the early 1970s, he had dropped the "System" from his recordings and continued to strive for broader success with various other bands.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: In 1973, he put together the Silver Bullet Band, a group of Detroit-area musicians, with whom he became most successful on the national level with the album Live Bullet, recorded live with the Silver Bullet Band in 1975 at Cobo Hall in Detroit, Michigan.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: In 1976, he achieved a national breakout with the studio album Night Moves.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: On his studio albums, he also worked extensively with the Alabama-based Muscle Shoals Rhythm Section, which appeared on several of Seger's best-selling singles and albums.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: A roots rocker with a classic raspy, shouting voice, Seger wrote and recorded songs that dealt with love, women and blue-collar themes and was an exemplar of heartland rock.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: Seger has recorded many hits, including "Night Moves", "Turn the Page", "We've Got Tonight", "Against the Wind", and "Shakedown" that was written for Beverly Hills Cop II and "Like a Rock", and also co-wrote the Eagles' number-one hit "Heartache Tonight".

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: His iconic recording of "Old Time Rock and Roll" was named one of the Songs of the Century in 2001.

title: Bob Seger
sentence_text: With a career spanning five decades, Seger continues to perform and record today.

title: Bob Seger

sentence_text: Grant is a disgruntled captain with the Beverly Hills Police Department who turns to trafficking drugs to obtain funds to support his life in the city .

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: Cade was shot in leg in the line of duty, a wound that almost broke his tibia.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: He was awarded a ribbon for his act of valor and a "pat on the back." He didn't feel this was sufficient compensation for his injury, so he grew bitter and resentful.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: He couldn't afford to live in the city he policed.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: He turned to a life of crime to support his lavish lifestyle, viewing it not as being traitorous, but getting what he deserved.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: He used this position as a Police Captain and Commander of BHPD Interdepartmental narcotics task force to aid criminals and sell drugs.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: During that time, his wife divorced him and his children won't speak to him, which was a result to focusing only on his job.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: Several months back, Detective Billy Rosewood made a bust on a Cartel crew out of Adelanto, though the case when nowhere and the charges were dropped for lack of evidence.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: The drugs Rosewood confiscated turned out to be powder.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: The supervisor on the case was Captain Grant, which solidified Rosewood's belief the Captain was dirty.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: He accused Grant of swapping out the drugs for the powder, and swore up and down to Chief Taggart that Grant was dirty.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: Taggart didn't believe him, and because Rosewood wouldn't let it go, he created enemies in the police department and ultimately turned in his badge.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: Grant worked with Lieutenant Copeland to traffic drugs through Beverly Hills .

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: Copeland wanted out of the deal and to go clean, but Grant couldn't risk him telling everyone the truth.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: When Copeland was undercover mulling drugs with cartel-adjacent member Sam Enriquez , Grant orchestrated a hit on Copeland that resulted in his murder.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: He then framed Enriquez for the crime.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: Rosewood didn't believe Enriquez was guilty, and tried to convince the detective on the case Bobby Abbott , though Abbott saw Rosewood as a crazy old detective interfering in his investigation.

title: Cade Grant
sentence_text: As such, Rosewood enlisted the help of his close friend and high-end criminal defense attorney Jane Saunders , who decided to represent Enriquez herself.
